message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-023 (fix) in src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
What was done: MlflowExperimentTracker.start_run terminates the created run FAILED if param-logging raises (no orphaned RUNNING run).
Regression test(s): test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

tests/test_observability.py:119:def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
75	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
76	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
77	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
78	        try:
79	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
80	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
81	        except Exception:
82	            try:
83	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
84	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
85	                pass
86	            raise
87	        return run_id
88	
89	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
90	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
91	
92	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
93	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
94	
95	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
96	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
97	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
98	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
99

1	"""Observability port — tracing + experiment tracking, all offline (no extra installed)."""
2	
3	import pytest
4	
5	from assay_engine._local import NonLocalEndpointError
6	from assay_engine.observability import tracing as tr
7	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
8	    OtelTracer,
9	    UnconfiguredTracer,
10	    bootstrap_tracing,
11	    run_trace_context,
12	    tracing_endpoint,
13	)
14	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import (
15	    MlflowExperimentTracker,
16	    get_tracking_uri,
17	)
18	
19	
20	# ---- tracing ----
21	
22	
23	def test_bootstrap_returns_none_when_disabled(monkeypatch):
24	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "1")
25	    assert bootstrap_tracing() is None
26	
27	
28	def test_bootstrap_returns_none_when_extra_absent(monkeypatch):
29	    # graceful degradation when the observability extra (phoenix) is not installed.
30	    import importlib.util
31	
32	    if importlib.util.find_spec("phoenix") is not None:
33	        pytest.skip("phoenix installed — real bootstrap is exercised in the integration suite")
34	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", raising=False)
35	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "_provider", None)
36	    assert bootstrap_tracing() is None
37	
38	
39	def test_bootstrap_returns_none_when_disabled_even_with_extra(monkeypatch):
40	    # the kill switch must win regardless of whether the extra is installed
41	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "1")
42	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "_provider", None)
43	    assert bootstrap_tracing() is None
44	
45	
46	def test_tracing_endpoint_is_loopback():
47	    assert tracing_endpoint().startswith("http://localhost:")
48	
49	
50	def test_tracing_endpoint_rejects_non_loopback(monkeypatch):
51	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "TRACING_HOST", "[REDACTED]")
52	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
53	        tracing_endpoint()
54	
55	
56	def test_run_trace_context_noop_without_run_id():
57	    with run_trace_context(None):
58	        pass  # must not raise
59	
60	
61	def test_run_trace_context_noop_without_otel():
62	    # opentelemetry absent -> the context manager is a safe no-op even with a run_id
63	    with run_trace_context("run-1"):
64	        pass
65	
66	
67	def test_otel_tracer_span_is_noop_without_otel():
68	    with OtelTracer().span("x", {"a": 1}):
69	        pass
70	
71	
72	def _in_memory_provider(monkeypatch):
73	    """Install a real in-memory OTel provider so emitted spans are inspectable."""
74	    from opentelemetry import trace
75	    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
76	    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import SimpleSpanProcessor
77	    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export.in_memory_span_exporter import InMemorySpanExporter
78	
79	    exporter = InMemorySpanExporter()
80	    provider = TracerProvider()
81	    provider.add_span_processor(SimpleSpanProcessor(exporter))
82	    # get_tracer_provider is read by OtelTracer; override the module-global for the test.
83	    monkeypatch.setattr(trace, "_TRACER_PROVIDER", provider, raising=False)
84	    monkeypatch.setattr(trace, "get_tracer_provider", lambda: provider)
85	    return exporter
86	
87	
88	def test_otel_span_records_error_status_and_exception_on_raise(monkeypatch):
89	    # #F-005 was a FALSE POSITIVE: start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and
90	    # set_status_on_exception=True, so an error escaping the span body IS auto-recorded as an
91	    # ERROR span with the exception event — no manual handling needed. This is a CHARACTERIZATION
92	    # guard: it pins the SDK behavior we rely on, so an accidental record_exception=False (or an
93	    # SDK default flip) is caught — errors must never render green/UNSET in Phoenix.
94	    from opentelemetry.trace import StatusCode
95	
96	    exporter = _in_memory_provider(monkeypatch)
97	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="boom"):
98	        with OtelTracer().span("phase:INGEST"):
99	            raise ValueError("boom")
100	    spans = exporter.get_finished_spans()
101	    assert len(spans) == 1
102	    assert spans[0].status.status_code is StatusCode.ERROR
103	    assert any(ev.name == "exception" for ev in spans[0].events)
104	
105	
106	def test_bootstrap_logs_warning_on_setup_failure(monkeypatch, caplog):
107	    # #F-044: a setup failure (e.g. bad host) must leave a diagnosable warning, not run silently
108	    # untraced.
109	    import logging
110	
111	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", raising=False)
112	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "_provider", None)
113	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "TRACING_HOST", "evil.example.com")  # non-loopback → require fails
114	    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger="assay_engine.observability"):
115	        assert bootstrap_tracing() is None
116	    assert any("setup failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
117	
118	
119	def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
120	    # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
121	    # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
122	    # requiring the mlflow extra.
123	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
124	    events = []
125	
126	    class _RunInfo:
127	        run_id = "run-xyz"
128	
129	    class _Run:
130	        info = _RunInfo()
131	
132	    class _FakeClient:
133	        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
134	            return None
135	
136	        def create_experiment(self, name):
137	            return "exp-1"
138	
139	        def create_run(self, exp_id, run_name=None):
140	            events.append(("create", run_name))
141	            return _Run()
142	
143	        def log_param(self, run_id, k, v):
144	            raise RuntimeError("store unreachable")
145	
146	        def set_terminated(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
147	            events.append(("terminated", run_id, status))
148	
149	    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
150	    monkeypatch.setattr(t, "_client", lambda: _FakeClient())
151	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="store unreachable"):
152	        t.start_run("study", {"k": "v"})
153	    assert ("terminated", "run-xyz", "FAILED") in events  # orphan run was not left RUNNING
154	
155	
156	def test_unconfigured_tracer_fails_loud():
157	    with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError):
158	        with UnconfiguredTracer().span("x"):
159	            pass
160	
161	
162	def test_install_flush_on_exit_wires_atexit_and_sigterm(monkeypatch):
163	    # span-flush on exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM must be wired and call force_flush/shutdown,
164	    # else buffered provenance spans are lost on container stop. No backend needed.
165	    import signal as _signal
166	
167	    calls = {"flush": 0, "shutdown": 0, "atexit": []}
168	
169	    class _Provider:
170	        def force_flush(self):
171	            calls["flush"] += 1
172	
173	        def shutdown(self):
174	            calls["shutdown"] += 1
175	
176	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: calls["atexit"].append(fn))
177	    captured = {}
178	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.signal, "getsignal", lambda s: _signal.SIG_DFL)
179	    monkeypatch.setattr(
180	        tr.signal, "signal", lambda sig, handler: captured.__setitem__("h", handler)
181	    )
182	
183	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(_Provider())
184	
185	    assert calls["atexit"], "atexit flush handler not registered"
186	    calls["atexit"][0]()  # simulate interpreter exit
187	    assert calls["flush"] >= 1
188	    if "h" in captured:  # SIGTERM handler installed (main thread)
189	        captured["h"](_signal.SIGTERM, None)
190	        assert calls["shutdown"] >= 1
191	
192	
193	def test_install_flush_on_exit_noop_without_force_flush(monkeypatch):
194	    # a provider lacking force_flush must not register anything (no crash)
195	    calls = []
196	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr.atexit, "register", lambda fn: calls.append(fn))
197	    tr._install_flush_on_exit(object())
198	    assert calls == []
199	
200	
201	# ---- experiment tracking ----
202	
203	
204	def test_bootstrap_is_idempotent(monkeypatch):
205	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", raising=False)
206	    sentinel = object()
207	    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "_provider", sentinel)
208	    assert bootstrap_tracing() is sentinel  # cached provider returned, not re-registered
209	
210	
211	def test_get_tracking_uri_resolves_sqlite_to_absolute_path(monkeypatch):
212	    import os
213	
214	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", raising=False)
215	    uri = get_tracking_uri()
216	    assert uri.startswith("sqlite:///")
217	    path = uri[len("sqlite:///") :]
218	    assert os.path.isabs(path)  # genuinely absolute, not a circular prefix check
219	
220	
221\[REDACTED](
222	    "uri",
223	    [
224	        "sqlite://evil.com/x.db",
225	        "//evil.com/share",
226	        "http://0.0.0.0:5000",
227	        "postgresql://db.example.com/m",
228	    ],
229	)

231	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", uri)

238	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):

243	    # constructs (validates local URI) even though mlflow is not installed
244	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", raising=False)

250	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", "postgresql://db.example.com/mlflow")
251	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
252	        MlflowExperimentTracker()
253	
254	
255	def test_tracker_start_run_fails_loud_without_mlflow(monkeypatch):
256	    import importlib.util
257	
258	    if importlib.util.find_spec("mlflow") is not None:
259	        pytest.skip("mlflow installed — this asserts the absent-extra fail-loud path")
260	    monkeypatch.delenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", raising=False)
261	    tracker = MlflowExperimentTracker()
262	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="observability' extra"):
263	        tracker.start_run("r", {})
264	
265	
266	def test_tracker_full_lifecycle_against_local_store(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
267	    # issue #O1: start_run -> log_metric -> log_artifact -> end_run must not collide on the
268	    # active-run stack. Runs only when the observability extra (mlflow) is installed.
269	    pytest.importorskip("mlflow")
270	    db = tmp_path / "mlflow.db"
271	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{db}")
272	    tracker = MlflowExperimentTracker(experiment="lifecycle-test")
273	    run_id = tracker.start_run("r1", {"alpha": "0.05"})
274	    tracker.log_metric(run_id, "score", 0.9)  # would raise on the old active-stack design
275	    artifact = tmp_path / "note.txt"
276	    artifact.write_text("hi")
277	    tracker.log_artifact(run_id, str(artifact))
278	    tracker.end_run(run_id)
279	    from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
280	
281	    run = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=f"sqlite:///{db}").get_run(run_id)
282	    assert run.data.metrics["score"] == 0.9
283	    assert run.info.status == "FINISHED"

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cp src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py /tmp/tracking_bak.py
.venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import re
p="src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py"
s=open(p).read()
# Replace the try/except param logging with naive version (no termination)
naive='''        for k, v in dict(params).items():
            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
        return run_id'''
import re
new=re.sub(r"        try:\n            for k, v in dict\(params\).items\(\):\n                client.log_param\(run_id, k, v\)\n        except Exception:\n            try:\n                client.set_terminated\(run_id, status=\"FAILED\"\)\n            except Exception:.*?\n                pass\n            raise\n        return run_id", naive, s, flags=re.S)
assert new!=s, "no substitution"
open(p,"w").write(new)
print("reverted")
EOF
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_observability.py::test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails -q 2>&1 | tail -8
cp /tmp/tracking_bak.py src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
echo "restored"

I verified by reverting the fix to a naive loop: the test fails on the `set_terminated` assertion. It is genuinely discriminating.

No concerns. Edge note (not blocking): the inner cleanup swallowing is intentional and well-commented; the original error is correctly re-raised.
